患有 ALS 的舞者如何利用脑电波进行现场表演

How a dancer with ALS used brainwaves to perform live

www.electronicspecifier.com 作者 1659447091 分数 34 评论 6 2026/04/12 10:09
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 2026 年 4 月 10 日
  2. 图片来源：Dentsu Creative/YouTube Dentsu Lab 的 Project Humanity 正在构建界面，将微小的肌肉信号和脑电波转化为完整的数字表达 - 并于 2025 年 12 月将其放在阿姆斯特丹的现场舞台上。我们构建的大多数产品中都嵌入了一个悄悄的假设：使用它们的人可以移动他们的手。他们可以滑动、点击、打字、点击。人机交互的整个范式都是围绕这个前提构建的。但对于全球估计有 2 亿患有严重身体残疾的人来说，情况并非如此。沉默中的信号 ALS（肌萎缩侧索硬化症）是一种进行性神经退行性疾病，负责自主运动的运动神经元逐渐退化。身体变得一动不动，通常是完全不动，而头脑却完好无损。对于大多数患者来说，这种疾病并不需要认知能力。需要表达。电通实验室的工程师将此视为接口问题。如果输出通道（身体）受到损害，那么解决方案就是寻找新的通道。他们的方法首先是将肌电图 (EMG) 传感器直接连接到患者的身体上。这些传感器可以检测到即使是最小的、最残余的肌肉收缩所产生的电活动。然后，原始生物数据被处理并映射到数字化身——虚拟空间中的全身表示，反映用户的预期动作。接口不是象征性的或抽象的；它被体现了。化身移动是因为该人的肌肉仍在尝试移动。该系统只是让这个意图变得清晰可见。不仅仅是可访问性 从技术上来说，这一点的有趣之处在于，化身不仅仅是一个化身——它还是一个控制界面。映射后，它可用于与任何软件交互：生产力工具、通信平台、创意应用程序。用户并不局限于定制的辅助工具。他们在数字空间中有一个通用的机构。电通实验室已经在电子竞技领域展示了这一点，举办了一些试验活动，让身体残疾的参与者与健全人一起在线玩游戏。结果是有启发性的：身体能力的差异在界面层变得无关紧要。这对游戏之外还有直接影响。如果该界面可以通用化，那么它就成为目前没有可行途径的人们进入元宇宙的途径——开放教育平台、远程工作环境和社交空间，这些都是其他方式无法访问的。阿姆斯特丹，2025 年 12 月：现场概念验证如果说电子竞技试验是功能证明，那么《Waves of Will》就是表达证明。 2025 年 12 月 10 日晚，Dentsu Lab 与 NTT Inc. 合作，在阿姆斯特丹举办了一场由患有 ALS 的专业舞者 Breanna Olson 主演的现场舞蹈表演。这里的技术机制从肌电图转向脑电波检测。 Breanna 的神经信号——与她移动、跳舞意图相对应的电活动——被实时捕获并转化为她的数字化身在舞台上表演的舞蹈。在接受 BBC 新闻采访时，布里安娜将这次经历描述为“令人兴奋”和“神奇”——看着自己以虚拟形式再次走上舞台。
  3. 上一篇文章
  4. 功率模块提高牵引逆变器的效率
  5. 2026 年 4 月 10 日 下一篇文章

Original Summary

  1. 10 April 2026
  2. Credit: Dentsu Creative/YouTube Dentsu Lab’s Project Humanity is building interfaces that translate minute muscle signals and brainwaves into full digital expression – and in December 2025, they put that on a live stage in Amsterdam. There is a quiet assumption embedded in most of the products we build: that the person using them can move their hands. They can swipe, tap, type, click. The entire paradigm of human-computer interaction has been built around that premise. But for an estimated 200 million people worldwide living with serious physical disabilities, that’s not the case. The signal in the silence ALS – amyotrophic lateral sclerosis – is a progressive neurodegenerative disease in which the motor neurons responsible for voluntary movement gradually degrade. The body becomes immobile, often entirely, while the mind remains fully intact. For most patients, the disease does not take cognition. It takes expression. The engineers at Dentsu Lab saw this as an interface problem. If the output channel – the body – is compromised, then the solution is to find a new one. Their approach begins with electromyography (EMG) sensors attached directly to the patient’s body. These sensors detect the electrical activity generated by even the smallest, most residual muscle contractions. That raw biological data is then processed and mapped onto a digital avatar – a full-body representation in virtual space that mirrors the user’s intended movements. The interface is not symbolic or abstract; it is embodied. The avatar moves because the person’s muscles are still trying to move. The system simply makes that intention legible. More than accessibility What makes this technically interesting is that the avatar is not just an avatar – it is a control surface. Once mapped, it can be used to interact with any software: productivity tools, communication platforms, creative applications. The user is not confined to a bespoke assistive tool. They have a general-purpose body in digital space. Dentsu Lab has already demonstrated this in an e-sports context, running trial events where participants with physical disabilities played games online alongside people without disabilities. The result was instructive: the differences in physical ability became irrelevant at the layer of the interface. This has direct implications beyond gaming. If the interface can be generalised, it becomes a pathway into the Metaverse for people who currently have no viable route in – opening up educational platforms, remote work environments, and social spaces that are otherwise inaccessible. Amsterdam, December 2025: a live proof of concept If the e-sports trial was a proof of function, Waves of Will was a proof of expression. On the evening of 10th December 2025, in collaboration with NTT Inc., Dentsu Lab staged a live dance performance in Amsterdam featuring Breanna Olson – a professional dancer who has been living with ALS. The technical mechanism here shifted from EMG to brainwave detection. Breanna’s neural signals – the electrical activity corresponding to her intention to move, to dance – were captured and translated in real time into choreography performed by her digital avatar on stage. In an interview with BBC News, Breanna described the experience as “exhilarating” and “magical” – watching herself, in virtual form, take to the stage once more.

  4. Power modules increase efficiency of traction inverters

评论（默认中文）

laxpri 2026/04/12 15:58
我理解这种情绪，但这看起来 50% 是假的（作为一个在 BCI 上进行自我研究的人），现在没有办法非侵入性设备可以实时做这么多事情，

他们的 yt 频道和编辑风格也证实了这一点。

但无论发生什么，我对这项技术持乐观态度，我相信有一天这肯定会改变人们的生活，但我们必须等到几年

I understand the emotion,but this looks 50 percent FAKE(as a person doing self research on BCI), there is no way right now that a Non-invasive device can do this much thing in real time,

Their yt channel and editing style also confirm that .

but what ever happens I am optimistic about this tech and I thing this is gonna change people life one day for sure , but we have to wait till few years

在 HN 中查看
usui 2026/04/12 15:16
该精选视频没有解释它如何使用信号来产生哪些结果，他们基本上只是说“我们在输出舞蹈时使用机器学习”。在 07:10，看起来这个人在“悲伤”和“如释重负”两个二元选项之间进行选择。不幸的是，我怀疑这个人对表演的实时投入是否达到了暗示的程度。电通也是日本的一家广告公司，所以看起来这更像是营销而不是技术。

真人的舞蹈总是事先编排好的，但现场表演总是表现出可以随时中断或改变为未经编排的动作的身体动作。我很难相信这个人的脑电波正在特定的 3D 空间中映射和生成全息图，而不是指示它在给定时间使用哪种情绪预设。

除了 ALS 故事的营销之外，我想我想知道它与迈克尔·杰克逊全息图表演有何不同，后者可以现场调整数学函数的滑块？

The featured video does not explain how it uses signals to produce which outcomes and they basically just say "we use machine learning while outputting a dance". At 07:10 it looks like the person chooses between two binary options of "sad" and "relieved". Unfortunately I doubt the person has anywhere near the real-time input to the performance as much as it is implied. Dentsu is also an advertisng agency in Japan, so it seems like this is more marketing than it is technical.

Dances by physical humans are always choreographed beforehand but live performances always show physical motion that can interrupt or change to unchoreographed movement at any time. I have a hard time believing that this person's brainwaves are mapping and producing the hologram in a specific 3D space, other than instructing it which mood preset to use at a given time.

Excluding the marketing of the ALS story, I guess I'm wondering how it's different from a Michael Jackson hologram performance where someone could adjust the sliders for mathematical functions live?

在 HN 中查看
EEBio 2026/04/12 15:32
我很确定你是对的，他们可能正在记录阿尔法波，可能与心率相结合。

从脑电图头皮记录中解码肢体关节运动基本上是一个未解决的问题（我们几乎无法在实验室中通过植入物做到这一点），我怀疑广告公司已经破解了它。

I am pretty sure you’re right, they are probably recording alpha waves, possibly combined with heart rate.

Decoding limb joint movements from EEG scalp recordings is basically an unsolved problem (we can barely do it in lab with implants), I doubt an advertising company has cracked it.

阿尔法波是什么意思，有什么好的文章吗...
what do you mean by alpha waves , is there any good article ...

MajorTakeaway 2026/04/12 11:21
现在正是做出贡献的好时机 https://openeeg.sourceforge.net/doc/ 就脑电图而言。通过脑电图可以观察到很多东西，老实说，随着时间的推移看到成长将是一件不错的事情。
Now is a really good time to contribute to https://openeeg.sourceforge.net/doc/ as far as EEG concerns go. There are a myriad of things that can be observed with EEG, and it would honestly be a decent thing to see grow in time.
在 HN 中查看
EEBio 2026/04/12 15:14
有很多针对不同范式的免费脑电图软件（其中之一是 MOABB - 所有 BCI 基准之母，并且有大量科学文章）。

对于业余爱好者来说，最大的瓶颈是，在使用脑电图时，大多数范例都需要昂贵的硬件才能工作，而且大多数范例仍然不能很好地处理实验室环境之外的头皮记录，即使使用中等成本的设备也是如此。

还有一个问题是，分类器通常必须非常简单，因为数据集很小，而且记录它们非常耗时（并且在删除嘈杂的纪元后，剩下的数据甚至更少）。跨课程和跨学科的学习很少起作用，因为脑电图取决于很多因素，如受试者的大脑解剖结构、电极的类型和精确位置、凝胶的数量（或缺乏）以及凝胶的干燥程度、受试者的情绪和注意力、影响受试者注意力的大量环境因素等等。

我见过的唯一比其他模式更可靠的范例是稳态视觉电位，因为您有不需要从脑电图学习的额外信息（视觉刺激的频率与受试者枕叶的频率大致相同）。

There is quite a number of freely available EEG software for different paradigms (one such collection is MOABB - Mother of All BCI Benchmarks, and there’s a huge number of scientific articles).

The biggest bottleneck for a hobbyist is that when using EEG, most paradigms require somewhat expensive hardware to work and that most paradigms still don’t work well with scalp recordings outside a lab environment, even when using mid-cost devices.

There’s also the issue that classifiers usually have to be quite simple because datasets are small, because they are time consuming to record (and after you remove noisy epochs, you have even less data left). Cross-session and cross-subject learning rarely works, since EEG is dependent on so many factors like subjects’ brain anatomy, the type and precise location of electrodes, amount of gel (or lack thereof) and how dried out it is, mood and focus of the subjects, a huge number of environmental factors that influence subjects’ focus and many others.

The only paradigm I have seen to work a bit more reliably than others is Steady State Visual Potentials, because you have extra information that doesn’t need to be learned from EEG (the frequency of visual stimuli is roughly the same as the one in subjects’ occipital lobe).
